narrator: Nora, an Alaska public-radio host: warm, grounded, quietly witty (Sulafat, warm)
notes: Style: conditional optimism, honestly bounded, warm and quietly witty, never hype and never doom. Arc: a bitter stuck argument, the machine arrives on the numbers, an honest deflation at the boom-up, a warm authorship turn at Redoubt, a genuine question button. Pace: BRISK and energetic, a sharp modern explainer; short natural breaths, no dragging; vary the tone line to line. Energy curve 4-3-2-3-2-4-3-4-2-4-3-4 so no two neighbors sit flat.
style_prompt:
Style: warm, grounded, quietly witty, conversational. This is conditional optimism, honestly bounded, not hype and not doom. Open on a bitter, stuck argument, two crowds shouting across one thin river; lean in, a little tired of it. Let one dry, wry half-smile land on the trawl fleet aside, then get honest again. Brighten and lift as the machine arrives, and let the numbers land, weeks, hours, eighty five thousand, ninety seven percent. Then deflate honestly and plainly on the line that they do not refill rivers; drop the energy, no snark. Turn warm and proud but not gushing at the Sitka weir, give weight to their river, their count. Close direct to camera, clean and warm, the final question genuinely asked and not rhetorical.
Pace: BRISK and energetic, keep it moving like a sharp modern explainer. Short natural breaths only, no long pauses, do not drag. Vary the tone line to line so no two sentences sound the same.
Emphasis: lean on the key word in each line; let numbers land.

lines.text: Two crowds keep shouting different numbers across one thin river.
Upriver, the smokehouses are quiet. The kings keep failing.
The trawl fleet [wry] hates salmon in its nets too. The ocean drives much of this.
Counting the catch took an observer weeks.
Now a Noah model reads the net cameras in hours.
Trained on eighty five thousand labeled fish. Ninety seven percent spotted. Still research.
This month a Senate bill demanded numbers everyone can trust.
The fight finally has a referee. But referees settle arguments. [short pause] They do not refill rivers.
At Redoubt Lake, the Sitka Tribe of Alaska built its own weir. Their river. Their count.
The machine won't refill the river. But whose count do you trust?

assembled_prompt:
Read ONLY the transcript below aloud as speech. The lines above "Transcript:" are direction; never speak them.
# AUDIO PROFILE: Nora, an Alaska public-radio host: warm, grounded, quietly witty (Sulafat, warm)
### DIRECTOR'S NOTES
Style: warm, grounded, quietly witty, conversational. This is conditional optimism, honestly bounded, not hype and not doom. Open on a bitter, stuck argument, two crowds shouting across one thin river; lean in, a little tired of it. Let one dry, wry half-smile land on the trawl fleet aside, then get honest again. Brighten and lift as the machine arrives, and let the numbers land, weeks, hours, eighty five thousand, ninety seven percent. Then deflate honestly and plainly on the line that they do not refill rivers; drop the energy, no snark. Turn warm and proud but not gushing at the Sitka weir, give weight to their river, their count. Close direct to camera, clean and warm, the final question genuinely asked and not rhetorical.
Pace: BRISK and energetic, keep it moving like a sharp modern explainer. Short natural breaths only, no long pauses, do not drag. Vary the tone line to line so no two sentences sound the same.
Emphasis: lean on the key word in each line; let numbers land.
Transcript:
[curious] Who counts this fish?
Two crowds keep shouting different numbers across one thin river.
Upriver, the smokehouses are quiet. The kings keep failing.
The trawl fleet [wry] hates salmon in its nets too. The ocean drives much of this.
Counting the catch took an observer weeks.
Now a Noah model reads the net cameras in hours.
Trained on eighty five thousand labeled fish. Ninety seven percent spotted. Still research.
This month a Senate bill demanded numbers everyone can trust.
The fight finally has a referee. But referees settle arguments. [short pause] They do not refill rivers.
[curious] So who holds the counter?
At Redoubt Lake, the Sitka Tribe of Alaska built its own weir. Their river. Their count.
The machine won't refill the river. But whose count do you trust?
